title: Mass. native’s 44-point performance helped Penn reach NCAA Tournament in dramatic fashion
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: T.J. Power scored a record 44 points leading Penn to an 88-84 overtime win against Yale in the Ivy League final.
The Massachusetts native secured an NCAA Tournament spot for Penn with his performance, hitting a crucial three-pointer in regulation.
Penn, a 14-seed, will face third-seeded Illinois in the NCAA Tournament's first round on Thursday at 9:25 p.m.

title: Hundreds of flights delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Over 300 flights were delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport due to severe weather, including high winds up to 70 mph.
Massport advises passengers to check their flight status before heading to the airport; delays and cancellations expected.
Airlines like Delta, Southwest, and American are offering itinerary changes without fees due to severe East Coast weather.

title: 11 charged after allegedly staging robberies to claim victim status on immigration applications
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 11 Indian nationals charged in Massachusetts for staging robberies to support fraudulent U Visa applications.
Seven suspects arrested in Massachusetts; others appeared in courts in Kentucky, Missouri, and Ohio.
Mastermind Rambhai Patel carried out staged robberies; sentenced to 20 months prison and $850,000 forfeiture.

title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: One person was injured in a South End shooting on Sunday evening, according to Boston Police Department reports.
The shooting occurred at approximately 7:42 p.m. near Washington Street and East Berkeley Street.
Police have made no arrests, with the investigation into the incident still ongoing.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony is emerging as a superstar at World Baseball Classic
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony hit two home runs, including a game-winning shot, for Team USA in the World Baseball Classic.
Through six WBC games, Anthony has a .318 average with two home runs and a 1.014 OPS.
Garrett Whitlock also excelled for Team USA, retiring the side in the eighth inning against the Dominican Republic.

title: 5 Boston sports moments that just missed out on the Soundbites Bracket
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rob Gronkowski's 'Yo soy fiesta' quote from 2012 AFC Championship falls short of Boston.com's top soundbites bracket.
Bill Belichick's 2015 'Mona Lisa Vito' quote excluded in favor of his more famous 'We’re on to Cincinnati' line.
Bill Lee's omission from the soundbites bracket attributed to lacking a standout quote despite his numerous colorful remarks.

title: Oscars 2026: The best jokes from Conan O’Brien’s monologue
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien hosted the 2026 Oscars at Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, delivering a humorous and high-energy monologue.
O'Brien's jokes included digs at Netflix CEO Ted Sarandos and a playful nod to future Oscars hosted by a Waymo.
Jimmy Kimmel joined O'Brien, introducing politically charged humor while presenting documentary categories.

title: Fundraiser: Community remembers Danvers mother, nurse killed in her home
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Janet Swallow, 68, was allegedly murdered in her Danvers home by high school senior Anthony DeMayo, who is facing charges.
A GoFundMe for Swallow's family has raised nearly $50,000, with a goal of $65,000 to cover emotional and financial burdens.
Swallow, a nurse at Lahey Hospital, is remembered for her kindness and impact, as her community mourns her loss.

title: Northeastern women’s hockey advances to Frozen Four after defeating Minnesota
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Northeastern women's hockey team advances to the Frozen Four after a 4-2 win against Minnesota.
Sophomore goalie Lisa Jönsson made 45 saves to secure the Huskies' victory over Minnesota.
Northeastern will face No. 1 Ohio State in the Frozen Four at Penn State on March 20.

title: Bomb threat reportedly halts plane at Logan ‘for hours’ before being deemed ‘not credible’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A JetBlue plane at Boston Logan Airport was delayed due to a bomb threat that was later deemed not credible.
The unidentified caller claimed a bomb was on a plane bound for Vero Beach, Florida, prompting a search by authorities.
FBI and bomb-sniffing dogs inspected the plane; passengers exited after luggage checks. No arrests have been made.
